steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rewrite $2x - 6y \gt  12$ as $x - 3y \gt  6$. A region is NOT in the solution set if no point in it can satisfy this inequality. Test each choice by checking the sign of $2x - 6y$ in that region.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \gt 0$: $2x \lt 0$ and $-6y \lt 0$, so $2x - 6y \lt 0 \lt 12$. The inequality can never be satisfied here. ✓
  • Choice B — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \lt 0$: $-6y \gt 0$, so $2x - 6y$ can be large and positive (e.g. $x = -1, y = -100$ gives $598 \gt 12$). ✗
  • Choice C — The region where $x \gt 0$ and $y \gt 0$: Large $x$ with small $y$ satisfies the inequality (e.g. $x = 10, y = 1$ gives $14 \gt 12$). ✗
  • Choice D — The $x$-axis: On the $x$-axis $y = 0$, so the inequality becomes $2x \gt 12 \Rightarrow x \gt 6$, which many points satisfy. ✗
That matches Choice A — The region where $x \lt 0$ and $y \gt 0$.
